primary_variables.matched_definition: Genetic perturbation that reduces or eliminates gene function.
evidence: To quantify changes in protein levels upon RPS26 silencing we applied SILAC in HEK293T cells
cells were transfected with siRPS26 or siCtrl in final concentration of 15 nM and harvested 48 hr post silencing
the silencing of another component of the 40 S subunit, the ribosomal protein RPS25, also induced repression of FMRpolyG biosynthesis

primary_variables.matched_definition: Genetic perturbation that reduces or eliminates gene function.
evidence: We demonstrated that depletion of RPS26 and its chaperone TSR2, modulates FMRpolyG production and its toxicity.
cells were transfected with siRPS26 or siCtrl in final concentration of 15 nM and harvested 48 hr post silencing

attributes.route_or_context: siRNA treatment in S-95xCGG and COS7 cells
evidence: in stable S-95xCGG model (Figure 5A) and COS7 cell line (Figure 5—figure supplement 1) treated with siRNA against TSR2 (siTSR2)

attributes.route_or_context: siRNA treatment in S-95xCGG and SH-SY5Y cells
evidence: Upon silencing of RPS25 in the stable S-95xCGG cell model
the reduction of RAN translation product was comparable after depletion of RPS25 or RPS26

replicate_hints: at least three independent biological replicates (N=3)
multiplex_channel_hints: SILAC in HEK293T cells grown in light and heavy amino acids (13C6 15N2 L-lysine-2HCl and 13C6 15N4 L-arginine-HCl)

evidence: The protein expression level in control and RPS26-depleted HEK293T cells was determined via quantitative mass spectrometry (SILAC-MS)

quantification_strategy: stable isotope labeling by amino acids in cell culture

base_candidates_ranked.reason: Explicitly describes SILAC in HEK293T cells using light and heavy amino acids (13C6 15N2 L-lysine and 13C6 15N4 L-arginine) for quantification of protein levels upon RPS26 silencing.
Explicitly mentions label-free quantification (LFQ) for the MS2 pull down samples (PXD047400) to identify proteins enriched on FMR1 RNA compared to GC-rich RNA.

trigger_keywords: stable isotope labeling with amino acids in cell culture
